source: Numerics/Data/Statistics/Probability.cs; adapted from the new v2.1.4 Test_Probability.cs Test_JointProbabilityHPCM_ExtremeProbabilitiesRemainFinite, which added the first upstream regression coverage for JointProbabilityHPCM.
reference: Numerics @ 2a0357a. Args convention (this port's own -- see fixtures/README.md and probability_hpcm_joint()/probability_hpcm_conditional_at() in core/tests/test_fixtures.cpp): args = [p_0..p_(n-1), ind_0..ind_(n-1), corr(n*n flattened row-major)] for hpcm_joint (n inferred from length); hpcm_conditional_at appends one trailing 0-based component index and returns the corresponding conditionalProbabilities[i] out-value. Reuses the upstream test's own inputs verbatim: probabilities [0, 1E-320, 0.5] (one exactly zero, one subnormal), indicators [1,1,1], and a 3x3 correlation matrix. v2.1.4 activated the `minimumCdf` (1E-300) underflow guard in JointProbabilityHPCM's 'First cycle' block, previously commented out there while already active in the 'Remaining cycles' loop three lines below -- see probability.hpp's file header (marked RESOLVED in docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md). Curating this case surfaced an UNRELATED, pre-existing precision bug in this port's own `Normal::standard_cdf` (a naive `0.5*(1+erf(z/sqrt2))` formula that catastrophically cancels to exactly 0.0/1.0 for |z|>~6, where the real C# `Normal.StandardCDF` delegates to the accurate MVNPHI algorithm instead) -- fixed in normal.hpp (see its file header) as a prerequisite for this fixture to reproduce at all, since JointProbabilityHPCM's z-values are clamped to [-9, 9] and these inputs drive z to exactly that boundary. Every value below was generated by running the case's inputs through tools/oracle_emitter --dump against the REAL C# Probability class and confirming the printed actuals reproduce via verify_oracles.py.

assertions.source: curated via oracle_emitter --dump; adapted from Test_JointProbabilityHPCM_ExtremeProbabilitiesRemainFinite (there only asserted finiteness and the [0,1] range; this port additionally pins the exact reproducible value)

curated via oracle_emitter --dump; adapted from Test_JointProbabilityHPCM_ExtremeProbabilitiesRemainFinite -- this is the conditionalProbabilities[0] entry that pins the normal.hpp standard_cdf precision fix directly: standard_cdf(-9) must return this value, not 0.0
